zh-tw.title: 治癒系語錄
zh-tw.description: 不催你振作，不逼你堅強。只是陪你坐一會兒，然後說：慢慢來也可以。

en.title: Healing Quotes
en.description: Not here to push you forward. Just here to sit with you for a moment and say: you're allowed to take your time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Taiwan healing content trend; 軟性療癒 format: gentle, non-preachy, permission-giving

zh-tw.content: 先好好活著，其他的事情可以慢慢來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 壓力很大但說不清楚從哪裡來的時候，這句話能讓人先停下來呼吸。適合傳給最近很辛苦但說沒事的朋友。
variations: 你不需要同時解決所有問題，一件一件來就好。

en.content: Start by just staying alive. Everything else can wait.
en.editorNote: When the pressure is overwhelming but you can't pinpoint why — this is the reminder to breathe first. Send to a friend who keeps saying 'I'm fine' when they clearly aren't.
variations: You don't have to solve everything at once. One thing at a time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by marieclaire.com.tw '現代人最需要的療癒金句'; original writing on permission to be tired

zh-tw.content:
你已經很努力了，累了就先停下來。
不是放棄，只是充電。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得「休息就是偷懶」的人。這句話是一個許可，適合傳給習慣撐著不說累的朋友，或是貼在自己桌面上。
variations: 電池沒電了需要充電，人也一樣，這不叫懶，叫必要。

en.content:
You've been trying hard enough. When you're tired, you're allowed to stop.
Not quitting — just recharging.
en.editorNote: For people who feel like resting is the same as giving up. This is permission. Good to send to a friend who never admits they're exhausted — or to keep for yourself.
variations: A phone with no battery needs charging. So do you. That's not laziness — that's maintenance.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by womany.net healing content; original writing on emotional permission and crying

zh-tw.content:
想哭的時候就哭吧。
眼淚不是軟弱，是你在幫自己清空空間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 習慣忍住不哭的人最需要聽這句。適合在深夜傳給對方，或是在自己快要撐不住的時候讀給自己聽。
variations: 哭完之後，那個空出來的位置，才能放進新的東西。

en.content:
If you need to cry, just cry.
Tears aren't weakness — they're how you clear space inside yourself.
en.editorNote: For people who hold it together until they can't. Good for late-night messages to someone struggling, or to read to yourself when you're barely holding on.
variations: After you cry, the space it clears is exactly where something new can grow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by healing drama quotes trend; original writing on imperfection as authenticity

zh-tw.content:
你不需要修好所有的裂縫。
有些縫隙，是光進來的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 完美主義者、或剛經歷失敗的人最需要聽這句。它說的是：你的不完整本身就有價值。適合搭配給剛失敗想重新出發的朋友。
variations: 金繼ぎ（日本金繼藝術）的哲學：把裂縫用金線填起來，壞掉的地方反而成了最美的部分。

en.content:
You don't need to seal every crack in yourself.
Some of them are where the light gets in.
en.editorNote: For perfectionists or anyone who just went through failure. The message: your brokenness has its own value. Good to send alongside something thoughtful to a friend starting over.
variations: Kintsugi — the Japanese art of repairing broken pottery with gold — says the same thing: the broken places become the most beautiful part.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by womany.net healing quotes; original writing on not owing anyone an explanation

zh-tw.content:
你的情緒不需要向任何人解釋。
難過就是難過，不需要有理由才算數。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些常常覺得自己難過「沒有資格」的人。情緒本身就是理由。也適合發給身邊常說「你沒道理這樣」的人看。
variations: 你不需要把傷心說得多慘才能被安慰，你難過就夠了。

en.content:
You don't owe anyone an explanation for how you feel.
Sad is sad. It doesn't need a reason to count.
en.editorNote: For people who feel like their sadness isn't 'valid enough.' Your feelings don't need to pass a threshold. Also useful to send to someone who keeps saying 'you have no reason to feel that way.'
variations: You don't need to make your pain sound bad enough to deserve comfort. Hurting is enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by gentle healing content; original writing on low periods being temporary

zh-tw.content:
低潮不是終點，只是你需要休息的路段。
停下來沒關係，不代表你不會再出發。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給正在低潮期的人。它不強迫你振作，只是說：停下來是被允許的。適合在深夜傳給正在難熬的朋友。
variations: 有些人走得很慢，但他們沒有停。你現在只是在補充能量。

en.content:
A low point isn't the end of the road — it's just the stretch where you need to rest.
Pulling over is okay. It doesn't mean you won't drive again.
en.editorNote: For someone in a rough patch. This doesn't push them to bounce back — it just says stopping is allowed. Good for a late-night message to a struggling friend.
variations: Some people move slowly but they never stopped. Right now you're just refueling.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 黃山料 healing quotes at eslite; original writing on protecting emotional energy

zh-tw.content:
你可以同情別人，但不需要把自己的能量全部給出去。
空了的杯子，倒不出任何東西給任何人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些習慣照顧所有人、卻忘了照顧自己的人。「情緒價值」很貴，自己也要留一份。適合傳給心地太好但常常累到不行的朋友。
variations: 先把自己的氧氣罩戴好，再去幫別人。這是飛機安全指引，也是人生道理。

en.content:
You can care about others without pouring yourself completely empty.
An empty cup has nothing to give anyone.
en.editorNote: For people who take care of everyone but forget themselves. Emotional energy is finite — keep some for yourself. Good for a kind-hearted friend who's always drained.
variations: Put your own oxygen mask on before helping others. That's airplane safety. It's also life advice.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by marieclaire.com.tw healing content; original writing on releasing things that don't fit

zh-tw.content:
放不下的東西，有時候是因為還沒做好離開的準備。
不用逼自己，時間會幫你的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給失戀或失去某段關係的人。這句話不催，只是說：你的步調沒有問題。適合傳給剛分手還在走出來的朋友。
variations: 有些告別需要說很多次，才能真的說完。

en.content:
Sometimes you can't let go because you're not ready to leave yet.
Don't force it. Time will help you get there.
en.editorNote: For someone recovering from a breakup or loss. This doesn't rush them — it just says their pace is okay. Good to send to a friend who's still processing.
variations: Some goodbyes need to be said more than once before they're really said.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 治癒系韓劇台詞 POPBEE 2026 article; original writing on not being hard on yourself

zh-tw.content:
你不需要對自己這麼嚴格。
你對別人的寬容，也可以分一點給自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給完美主義者或習慣自我批判的人。這句話讓人意識到，他們對自己的標準往往比對別人嚴苛得多。適合晨間自我提醒。
variations: 如果朋友犯了你犯的錯，你會怎麼跟他說？那就也這樣對自己說。

en.content:
You don't have to be this hard on yourself.
The grace you extend to others — give yourself some of that too.
en.editorNote: For perfectionists and chronic self-critics. It highlights how much harsher we are to ourselves than to the people we love. Good for a morning reminder.
variations: If a friend made the same mistake you just made, what would you say to them? Say that to yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by womany.net healing content; original writing on relationships ending without blame

zh-tw.content:
有些人離開不是因為誰的錯，
只是這段路，走到他們該轉彎的地方了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給因為關係結束而不斷自問「是不是我哪裡不好」的人。有時候結束就只是結束，不需要找出一個犯錯的人。適合分手後或友誼淡化後。
variations: 不是所有的結束都需要一個理由，有些事情就是它的時間到了。

en.content:
Sometimes people leave not because anyone did something wrong —
just because the road brought them to a place where they had to turn.
en.editorNote: For someone who keeps asking 'what did I do wrong' after a relationship ends. Sometimes it just ends. No one has to be the villain. Good for breakups or drifting friendships.
variations: Not every ending needs a reason. Some things just reach their time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by marieclaire.com.tw; original writing on small steps mattering

zh-tw.content:
今天能做到的，可能只是起床、吃飯、呼吸。
那也算是今天的完成了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給最低潮、連基本事情都很難做到的人。這句話說的是：你的努力是相對的，今天就這樣也可以。適合在深夜傳給正在掙扎的人。
variations: 某些天，不崩潰就是全部了。

en.content:
Some days, all you can manage is getting up, eating, and breathing.
That still counts as finishing today.
en.editorNote: For people at their lowest, when even basic tasks feel impossible. The message: your effort is relative to where you are right now, and that's okay. Good for a late-night message to someone who's really struggling.
variations: Some days, not falling apart is the whole achievement.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by healing drama quotes trend; original writing on not rushing recovery

zh-tw.content:
不用急著好起來。
修復需要時間，快不快不是重點，方向才是。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些因為「還沒走出來」而焦慮的人。這句話說的是：康復沒有期限，你沒有落後。適合傳給因為恢復速度太慢而自責的朋友。
variations: 傷口的癒合有它自己的時程，不是你能催的。

en.content:
You don't have to hurry up and heal.
Recovery takes time. The pace doesn't matter — the direction does.
en.editorNote: For people anxious about 'not being over it yet.' The message: healing has no deadline and you're not behind. Good for a friend who's beating themselves up about recovering too slowly.
variations: A wound heals on its own schedule. You can't rush it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by womany.net healing quotes; original writing on protecting your peace from toxic patterns

zh-tw.content:
對你不好的事，不一定要撐著。
離開也是一種照顧自己的方式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些明知道某段關係或某個環境不對，但還是硬撐的人。離開不是逃跑，是自我保護。適合在需要做出決定但還在猶豫的時候讀。
variations: 有些門你一直推，是因為它本來就是要拉的。換個方向就好了。

en.content:
You don't have to keep enduring things that are hurting you.
Leaving is also a way of taking care of yourself.
en.editorNote: For people staying in a situation they know isn't right for them. Leaving isn't running away — it's self-preservation. Good to read when you're facing a hard decision.
variations: Some doors you keep pushing are meant to be pulled. Just change direction.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by eslite healing quotes; original writing on being a safe place for yourself

zh-tw.content:
如果外面的世界太吵，
至少讓自己的內心成為一個安靜的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給覺得到處都是壓力、找不到喘息空間的人。這句話提醒你，內在的平靜是可以被練習的，不需要等外部條件變好。適合用在冥想或睡前的時刻。
variations: 你可以在世界最吵的地方找到屬於你自己的安靜。

en.content:
When the outside world gets too loud,
at least make your inner world a quiet place.
en.editorNote: For people who feel pressure everywhere and can't find space to breathe. Inner peace can be practiced — it doesn't wait for external conditions to improve. Good for meditation or bedtime.
variations: You can find your own quiet in the loudest place in the world.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by POPBEE 2026 healing content; original writing on existing being enough

zh-tw.content:
你不需要一直有用、一直有成績、一直被需要。
你只是存在著，就已經夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些把「有沒有用」當成自我價值標準的人。這句話說的是：你的存在本身就有意義，不需要一直表現給誰看。
variations: 你是人，不是工具。存在本身就是一件完整的事。

en.content:
You don't always have to be useful, productive, or needed.
Just existing — that's already enough.
en.editorNote: For people who measure their worth by how much they contribute or how much they're needed. The message: you matter because you exist, not because of what you produce.
variations: You are a person, not a tool. Simply being here is already complete.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by innertune.com self-forgiveness collection; original writing on releasing guilt toward yourself

zh-tw.content:
你願意原諒別人的錯，
也可以試著原諒當時還不夠好的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些對過去的決定或行為反覆懊悔的人。原諒自己不是替自己找藉口，而是允許自己繼續往前走。適合在深夜反覆回想過去的時候讀。
variations: 那個時候的你，用那個時候能有的方式做了決定。現在的你已經不一樣了。

en.content:
You're willing to forgive others for their mistakes.
You can try extending that same grace to the version of you who didn't know better yet.
en.editorNote: For people who replay past decisions with guilt and regret. Forgiving yourself isn't making excuses — it's giving yourself permission to keep going. Good to read on those late-night spirals.
variations: The version of you back then made the best decision they could with what they had. You're different now.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by healingbrave.com forgiveness and growth themes; original writing on trusting yourself again after failure

zh-tw.content:
跌倒過不代表你走路有問題，
只是那條路剛好不平。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給因為失敗而開始懷疑自己能力的人。失敗是情境，不是性格。適合傳給剛在工作或考試中受挫的朋友，幫他們把眼光放回自己身上而不是那次的結果。
variations: 你沒有問題，只是那件事的時機、條件剛好沒對上。

en.content:
Falling doesn't mean something is wrong with how you walk.
That stretch of road was just uneven.
en.editorNote: For people who start doubting their own ability after a failure. Failure is a situation, not a character trait. Good for a friend who just had a rough setback at work or school — helps redirect focus from the outcome back to themselves.
variations: Nothing's broken in you. The timing and conditions just weren't lined up this time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by anniewright.com uplifting quotes; original writing on hard experiences building depth

zh-tw.content:
不是每一段辛苦都有明顯的意義，
但它們都在悄悄地讓你變得更厚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給正在熬一段不知道目的在哪的辛苦的人。這句話不是在說「吃苦當吃補」，而是說：就算看不出來，這段經歷也在改變你。適合在陷入懷疑的時候給自己看。
variations: 你現在說不出這段日子有什麼用，但幾年後回頭看，你會懂。

en.content:
Not every hard stretch has an obvious purpose.
But all of them quietly make you more substantial.
en.editorNote: For people grinding through a period that doesn't seem to lead anywhere. This isn't 'suffering builds character' preachiness — it's just saying: even if you can't see it, this experience is changing you. Good for self-reading during a moment of doubt.
variations: You might not be able to explain what this period was for right now. A few years from now, you'll know.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by cmykhuang.com 台灣心靈語錄; original writing on beginning again being an act of courage

zh-tw.content:
重新開始不是失敗的證明，
是你還願意再試一次的勇氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得「重來」很丟臉、或是不敢承認自己需要從頭開始的人。重來是需要勇氣的，不是弱點。適合在人生轉換點、換工作、重啟一段關係時讀。
variations: 每一個重新開始，都是你沒有放棄自己的證據。

en.content:
Starting over isn't proof that you failed.
It's proof that you still have the courage to try again.
en.editorNote: For people who feel embarrassed about 'starting over,' or who can't bring themselves to admit they need to begin again. Starting fresh takes guts — it isn't weakness. Good for life transitions: a new job, ending something, or beginning again.
variations: Every time you start over, it's evidence that you haven't given up on yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by POPBEE 2026 healing drama quotes; original writing on collecting small ordinary moments of happiness

zh-tw.content:
不用等到一切都好了才快樂。
今天的一杯熱咖啡、一個好天氣，也算。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得要等「大事情」發生才能感覺幸福的人。快樂可以很小、很近。適合在日常疲憊時傳給自己或朋友，提醒彼此留意身邊的小事。
variations: 幸福不是一個目的地，而是你今天注意到的那些小東西。

en.content:
You don't have to wait until everything is better to feel okay.
A warm drink, a stretch of good weather today — those count too.
en.editorNote: For people waiting on some big event before they let themselves feel happy. Joy can be small and close. Good to send to yourself or a friend as a reminder to notice the little things around them.
variations: Happiness isn't a destination. It's whatever small thing you noticed today.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion quote collections; original writing on extending to yourself the kindness you give friends

zh-tw.content:
你對自己說話的方式，
也應該是你對最好的朋友說話的方式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些對自己說話很嚴厲、卻對朋友無比溫柔的人。試試看把那份溫柔也留一點給自己。適合貼在鏡子上或桌面作為每日提醒。
variations: 如果你對朋友不會說那些話，那就也別對自己說。

en.content:
The way you talk to yourself
should be the same way you'd talk to your closest friend.
en.editorNote: For people who are harsh with themselves but endlessly gentle with others. Try keeping a little of that gentleness for yourself. Good as a daily reminder — stick it on a mirror or a lock screen.
variations: If you wouldn't say it to a friend, don't say it to yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by deliberatedirections.com healing and recovery quotes; original writing on healing being quiet and unhurried

zh-tw.content:
療癒很多時候是安靜的。
沒有什麼戲劇性的轉折，只是某天早上，你發現自己呼吸比以前順了一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得「還沒好起來」的人。療癒不是突然發生的，它是一點一點在你沒注意的時候進行的。適合在覺得沒有進展時讀給自己聽。
variations: 你以為自己還在原地，但其實你已經走了很遠了。

en.content:
Healing is often quiet.
No dramatic turning point — just one morning you realize breathing feels a little easier than it used to.
en.editorNote: For people who feel like they still haven't gotten better. Healing doesn't happen all at once — it happens gradually, when you're not watching. Good to read on days when it feels like nothing is changing.
variations: You think you're standing still. But you've actually come a long way.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by innertune.com healing quotes collection; original writing on choosing rest as an active decision, not passivity

zh-tw.content:
選擇休息，不是放棄努力，
是你決定要好好保護做決定的那個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些把休息等同於「輸了」的人。休息是策略，不是投降。適合傳給連假日都在工作、卻還是覺得自己不夠努力的朋友。
variations: 你保護好自己，才有辦法繼續保護你在乎的事情。

en.content:
Choosing to rest isn't giving up on trying.
It's deciding to protect the person who makes all your choices.
en.editorNote: For people who equate rest with losing. Rest is strategy, not surrender. Good for a friend who works through weekends and still feels like they're not doing enough.
variations: Taking care of yourself is what lets you keep taking care of everything else that matters to you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by mindfulnessexercises.com healing quotes; original writing on small daily acts of peace as real healing

zh-tw.content:
每一次你選擇不繼續糾結，
每一次你讓自己好好吃頓飯、好好睡一覺，
都是在認真療癒自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得「療癒」很大、很遙遠的人。其實每一個小小的選擇都算。適合在睡前讀，提醒自己今天做到的那些小事都有意義。
variations: 療癒不是某一天突然發生的事，是你今天做的每一個小決定。

en.content:
Every time you choose to stop obsessing over something,
every time you let yourself eat a proper meal or get a full night of sleep —
that's real healing.
en.editorNote: For people who think healing has to be big or far away. Every small choice counts. Good to read before bed as a reminder that the little things you did today all meant something.
variations: Healing isn't something that happens to you one day. It's every small decision you make today.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by quotena.com self-healing quotes 2026; original writing on damage no longer having control over your life

zh-tw.content:
那些傷還在，但它們不再是你的方向盤了。
你可以帶著它們，繼續往你想去的地方走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些以為「沒有完全好起來就不能繼續往前」的人。傷口不需要消失，只需要不再掌控你。適合給正在走出一段很深的痛的朋友。
variations: 你不需要假裝傷口不存在，只需要讓它不再決定你是誰。

en.content:
The wounds are still there — but they're not in the driver's seat anymore.
You can carry them with you and still go where you want to go.
en.editorNote: For people who feel like they can't move forward until they're fully healed. The wound doesn't have to disappear — it just can't be in control. Good for a friend working through deep grief or loss.
variations: You don't have to pretend the hurt isn't there. You just don't have to let it decide who you are.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by calmer-you.com self-care quotes; original writing on asking for help being a form of strength

zh-tw.content:
說出「我需要幫忙」，
不是承認自己不夠好，
是你終於願意讓人靠近了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些習慣一個人扛、開不了口求助的人。開口需要比沉默更大的勇氣。適合在意識到自己撐不下去的那一刻讀，也可以傳給還在硬撐的朋友。
variations: 讓別人幫你，也是給對方一個機會。

en.content:
Saying 'I need help'
isn't admitting you're not enough —
it's finally letting someone in.
en.editorNote: For people who carry everything alone and can't bring themselves to ask. Asking out loud takes more courage than staying silent. Good to read in the moment you realize you can't hold it alone anymore — or to send to a friend who's still white-knuckling it.
variations: Letting someone help you is also giving them the chance to show up for you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by solightlyliving.com emotional healing quotes; original writing on sitting with uncomfortable feelings instead of running

zh-tw.content:
不舒服的感覺不需要馬上被解決。
有時候只要讓它在那，坐一會兒，它就會自己慢慢散開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些一有不舒服就想立刻逃開或壓下去的人。與其對抗情緒，不如先讓它存在。適合在焦慮或煩躁突然湧上來、卻找不到原因的時候讀。
variations: 你不用趕走每一個壞感覺，有些只是需要被看見一下。

en.content:
An uncomfortable feeling doesn't need to be fixed right away.
Sometimes you just let it be there for a while, and it slowly dissolves on its own.
en.editorNote: For people who immediately try to escape or suppress anything that feels bad. Instead of fighting the emotion, just let it exist. Good for when anxiety or unease hits out of nowhere and you can't figure out why.
variations: You don't have to chase away every bad feeling. Some just need to be noticed for a moment.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by bohemia.tw 心情語錄; original writing on the quiet dignity of carrying on through hard days

zh-tw.content:
有些天，你只是撐過去了。
沒有什麼偉大的領悟，沒有轉捩點。
但你撐過去了，這件事本身就很了不起。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些熬過了很平凡卻很沉重的一天的人。不是所有的堅強都很戲劇化，默默撐著也算。適合在結束了一個特別難熬的普通日子之後讀。
variations: 不是每一天都有意義，但每一天都是你走過來的。

en.content:
Some days you just got through it.
No big epiphany, no turning point.
But you got through it — and that alone is something.
en.editorNote: For people who survived an ordinary but very heavy day. Not all resilience looks dramatic. Quietly enduring counts too. Good to read at the end of a day that felt hard for no obvious reason.
variations: Not every day has to mean something. But every day is one you made it through.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by cmykhuang.com 心靈語錄; original writing on the relationship between present-moment awareness and emotional peace

zh-tw.content:
你不需要把明天的事情搬到今天來扛。
今天的你，只需要負責今天就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給容易把未來的擔憂全部壓在當下的人。這句話不是叫你不要計畫，而是說：現在這一刻不需要承擔所有的不確定。適合在焦慮睡不著的夜晚讀。
variations: 明天有明天要面對的事，今晚的你只需要好好休息。

en.content:
You don't need to carry tomorrow's weight today.
The only thing you're responsible for right now is right now.
en.editorNote: For people who drag all their future worries into the present moment. This isn't telling you not to plan — it's saying this moment doesn't have to hold all the uncertainty at once. Good to read on anxious nights when sleep won't come.
variations: Tomorrow has its own things to face. Tonight, your only job is to rest.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by zencare.co mental health quotes collection 2025; original writing on vulnerability being a form of courage, not weakness

zh-tw.content:
讓別人看見你不完整的樣子，
不是示弱，是你夠勇敢了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些習慣把所有柔軟都藏起來、只讓人看見自己堅強那面的人。真正的勇氣有時候是：讓人看見你的不確定。適合在試著對身邊的人開口之前讀。
variations: 讓人靠近你需要比任何事都更大的勇氣。

en.content:
Letting someone see your unfinished edges
isn't weakness — it means you were brave enough.
en.editorNote: For people who hide every soft part and only show strength. Real courage sometimes looks like letting someone see your uncertainty. Good to read before trying to open up to someone close to you.
variations: Letting people come close takes more courage than almost anything else.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by mindfulnessexercises.com self-care quotes; original writing on inner peace being independent of external circumstances

zh-tw.content:
外面的事你不一定控制得了，
但你可以練習：不讓它把你整個人都帶走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些習慣讓外部事件完全左右自己心情的人。這句話說的不是冷漠，而是有意識地為自己的內心留一塊地方。適合在剛被某件事情影響心情之後讀。
variations: 你可以在意一件事，但不需要讓它佔據你的全部。

en.content:
You can't always control what's happening out there.
But you can practice not letting it take over everything inside you.
en.editorNote: For people who let external events fully hijack their mood. This isn't about becoming cold — it's about consciously protecting a corner of your inner world. Good to read right after something has knocked you off balance.
variations: You can care about something without letting it consume all of you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by seekingbetterbalance.com boundary quotes; original writing on boundaries expanding rather than limiting your life

zh-tw.content:
說「不」不是自私，
是你在告訴自己：我的時間和精神也很重要。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些說不出「不」、害怕讓人失望的人。拒絕不是冷漠，而是你在認真對待自己的有限資源。適合在又一次答應了一件不想做的事之後讀。
variations: 每次你說了一個不夠真心的「好」，你就欠了自己一點。

en.content:
Saying no isn't selfish.
It's telling yourself: my time and energy matter too.
en.editorNote: For people who can't say no and are terrified of disappointing others. Declining isn't coldness — it's taking your own limited resources seriously. Good to read after you've agreed to something you really didn't want to do.
variations: Every time you say a yes you don't mean, you owe yourself a little something.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by makeheadway.com self-worth quotes; original writing on self-worth existing independent of achievement or approval

zh-tw.content:
你的價值不是你完成了多少事情算出來的。
那是你本來就有的東西，不需要靠表現去證明。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些把成就感跟自我價值綁在一起、沒達成目標就覺得自己不夠好的人。你的價值在任何結果出現之前就存在了。適合在成績或結果讓你失望之後讀。
variations: 你不需要今天有什麼成果，才算是有意義的人。

en.content:
Your worth isn't calculated by how much you've accomplished.
It's something you already had — before any result came in.
en.editorNote: For people who tie their self-worth to achievement and feel inadequate when they fall short of a goal. Your value existed before any outcome. Good to read after a result or score has left you feeling like you're not enough.
variations: You don't need to produce something today to be a person who matters.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by ryanzofay.com mental health quotes; original writing on growth happening gradually and invisibly

zh-tw.content:
你以為自己沒有在進步，
但進步很多時候是無聲的。
某天你回頭看，才發現走了這麼遠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些因為看不到立即的改變而懷疑自己有沒有在成長的人。改變不是直線進行的，也不一定有清楚的時間點。適合在覺得停滯不前、快要放棄的時候讀。
variations: 沒有感覺到在動，不代表沒有在走。

en.content:
You think you're not making progress.
But progress is often silent.
One day you look back and realize how far you've actually come.
en.editorNote: For people who doubt their own growth because they can't see immediate change. Growth doesn't move in a straight line, and it rarely announces itself. Good to read when you feel stuck and are close to giving up.
variations: Not feeling like you're moving doesn't mean you're standing still.
